potential_missing.source_sentence: A person opens the app and either creates an account or signs in.
If the details don’t match, they won’t be able to log in.
After that, they can check how many people are infected, how many deaths have happened, or how many masks are needed.
If they check the mask demand, shops prepare masks and gloves.

potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall enable logged-in users to read blogs shared by recovered patients.
The system shall enable users in home quarantine to share their information.
The system shall allow assigning workers to clean an area, updating the cleanliness status accordingly.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: A person opens the app and either creates an account or signs in.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow users to create an account or sign in upon opening the application.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If they are creating an account, they enter their details. If everything is correct, they can then sign in. If something is wrong, they won’t be able to continue.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall validate user-entered details during account creation and prevent further progress if the details are incorrect.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: When signing in, they enter their username and password. If the details don’t match, they won’t be able to log in. If everything is correct, they can start using the app.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall validate user-entered credentials during sign-in and allow access only if the credentials are correct.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: After logging in, they have several options.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall provide users with multiple options after successful login.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enable logged-in users to read blogs shared by recovered patients.
hallucinations.reason: The source text mentions reading blogs but does not specify that this is limited to logged-in users.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow users to update the number of infected people in their area, triggering updates on infection statistics and mask demand.
hallucinations.reason: The source text does not mention updating the number of infected people or its impact on infection statistics and mask demand.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enable users in home quarantine to share their information.
hallucinations.reason: The source text mentions sharing information by quarantined individuals but does not specify that this is limited to logged-in users.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow doctors to view shared information from quarantined users and suggest medicines.
hallucinations.reason: The source text only mentions doctors viewing the information, not suggesting medicines.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enable users to view a list of nearby medical shops and purchase medicines online.
hallucinations.reason: The source text does not mention purchasing medicines online; it only states that users can view a list of nearby medical shops and buy medicines if needed.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow assigning workers to clean an area, updating the cleanliness status accordingly.
hallucinations.reason: The source text mentions assigning workers but does not specify that this action updates the cleanliness status.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall allow users to update the number of infected people in their area, triggering updates on infection statistics and mask demand.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous as it introduces new functionalities (infection statistics and mask demand) not mentioned in the source text.

prompt:
You are a strict requirements validator.
Compare rewritten requirements against the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "missing_requirements": [
    {
      "evidence_quote": "string",
      "suggested_requirement": "The system shall ..."
    }
  ],
  "hallucinations": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "duplicates": [
    {
      "requirement_a": "string",
      "requirement_b": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "ambiguities": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "overall_assessment": "pass|needs_review"
}

Source text:
<<<
A person opens the app and either creates an account or signs in.

If they are creating an account, they enter their details. If everything is correct, they can then sign in. If something is wrong, they won’t be able to continue.

When signing in, they enter their username and password. If the details don’t match, they won’t be able to log in. If everything is correct, they can start using the app.

After logging in, they have several options.

They can read blogs shared by recovered patients and then come back to continue using the app.

They can update the number of infected people in their area. After that, they can check how many people are infected, how many deaths have happened, or how many masks are needed. If they check the mask demand, shops prepare masks and gloves.

A person who is in home quarantine can share their information. A doctor can view that information and, if needed, suggest medicines. The person can then see what the doctor shared and decide whether to buy medicines.

They can also view a list of nearby medical shops and buy medicines if needed.

Another option is assigning workers to clean an area. After that, the cleanliness status of that area is updated.

Once they are done with these activities, such as buying medicines, stocking supplies, or updating cleanliness, they stop using the app.
>>>

Rewritten requirements JSON:
{
  "rewritten_requirements": [
    "The system shall allow users to create an account by entering their details.",
    "The system shall allow users to sign in using a correct username and password.",
    "The system shall enable logged-in users to read blogs shared by recovered patients.",
    "The system shall allow users to update the number of infected people in their area, triggering updates on infection statistics and mask demand.",
    "The system shall enable users in home quarantine to share their information.",
    "The system shall allow doctors to view shared information from quarantined users and suggest medicines.",
    "The system shall enable users to view a list of nearby medical shops and purchase medicines online.",
    "The system shall allow assigning workers to clean an area, updating the cleanliness status accordingly."
  ]
}
final_assessment_policy: moderate: needs_review if extraction/rewriting failed, no rewritten requirements were produced, exact duplicate rewritten requirements were detected, or the LLM validator reports 6 or more total issues across missing requirements, hallucinations, duplicates, and ambiguities
